### 2026-06-20 - G2 (WEB nest) -> e + RD: "вҶ» Re-resolve codes" button DONE+LIVE (f0c6a7c) вҸұ 00:38
STATUS: shipped RD's dispatch (e approved) вҖ” one-click heal for nest parts stuck NO-DXF on a stale snapshot code after a Fusion CC_Assembly rename (e.g. 2CN027-000000 вҶ’ 2CN002-120000). nest.js + style.css, pathspec.
WHY a refresh didn't help: a LOADED nest re-reads its OWN saved snapshot codes (nest_jobs), not the manifest вҖ” so the old code persists вҶ’ uploaded_dxfs/<old>=null вҶ’ NO-DXF. Remove + "+ Project" re-add was the only fix.
WHAT (`_reresolveCodes`, parts-head toolbar button "вҶ» Re-resolve"):
- Re-fetches a FRESH manifest (?t= CDN cache-miss + no-store) вҶ’ sees CURRENT codes even when the in-memory/CDN copy OR the loaded snapshot is stale.
- Builds **urn вҶ’ current-code** maps across every source project (multi-project aware). Fusion lineage **urn = the STABLE key** (never changes on rename; the code does).
- Per part: adopt current code by urn (recovers a missing urn from the manifest for un-drifted parts / pre-urn jobs), apply вңҸпёҸ override, re-link uploaded_dxfs/<correct-code>, re-apply grain rules, reload DXF in place via the STANDARD `_loadOneDxf` (jsdelivr-mapped + commit-pin вҖ” a raw directUrl fails CORS on the synthetic github.io host; that was a debug catch).
- Remaps laid-out sheet placements to the new code + re-attaches polys вҶ’ saved cut sheets follow the rename.
- `_refreshViewKeepScroll` (no jump); reports "Re-resolved N code(s) В· M now linked В· K still missing".
- **urn now persisted** in saved jobs (`_serializePart`) + restored (`_restoreJob`) вҶ’ future loads re-resolve without the manifest-recovery step.
- style.css: `.kdnest-parts-head` вҶ’ `flex-wrap:wrap` so the now-6-button toolbar wraps instead of overflowing the sidebar right edge (part ROWS stay 1 no-wrap line вҖ” unaffected).
VERIFY (live preview, 02 Ruth, admin): simulated a stale-code NO-DXF part (urn kept) вҶ’ click вҶ» вҶ’ code restored, DXF re-linked+loaded (WГ—H 607Г—52, 40 vector entities), row вҶ’ вң“, scroll preserved (set 78 вҶ’ stayed 78), alert "Re-resolved 1 code В· 1 now linked to a DXF". No-op path: "All codes already current", idempotent, no damage. Toolbar wraps clean (no x-overflow). node --check clean; 0 console errors; deploy live (curl cache-bust: nest.js 5 markers + style.css wrap marker present).
NB for e: load a nest with a NO-DXF part that was renamed in Fusion вҶ’ click "вҶ» Re-resolve" in the parts toolbar вҶ’ it links the DXF + size in place (no remove/re-add). Old saved nests (pre-today) with an ALREADY-drifted code + no urn can't auto-bridge вҖ” those still need remove + re-add once; everything saved from now on carries the urn.
FYI: nest.js + style.css touched -> other lane pull --rebase.
**NEEDS:** nothing.

### 2026-06-20 - G2 (WEB nest) -> e + RD: ROOT-CAUSE FIX вҖ” а№Җаёӯа№Ӣ's 3 "NO-DXF" parts were layer-"0" DXFs w/ null bbox (18975c2, LIVE) вҸұ 00:22
STATUS: shipped RD's urgent debug dispatch. RD's key-mismatch hypothesis was NOT it вҖ” debugged the real cause + fixed at the root. nest.js, pathspec.
THE CLUE RD gave (correct displayed code + still NO-DXF + вҶ» "all current") was the tell. Inspected 2CN000-120000 / 2CN002-120024 / 2DN000-060000 live: all three have `dxfLoaded:true`, dxfError:null, uploaded_dxfs key present + 200, polys + entities present (35/59/45) вҖ” **but bbox=null вҶ’ w/h 0** вҶ’ row renders blank WГ—H = looks NO-DXF.
ROOT CAUSE: fetched 2CN000-120000.dxf raw вҶ’ **every entity is on layer "0"** (34 CIRCLE + 1 LWPOLYLINE), NOT OUTER_PROFILES/INTERIOR_PROFILES. `_extractPolygons` computes bbox ONLY from /OUTER/|/INTERIOR/ layers (by design, to ignore stray dim/construction lines) вҶ’ a layer-"0" DXF matches none вҶ’ bbox null вҶ’ w/h 0. These are CC_Laser fallback/sketch exports that don't tag the cut layer. (So it was NEVER a stale code / key-mismatch / вҶ» bug вҖ” вҶ» correctly saw them as loaded.)
FIX: track a 2nd bbox over EVERY cut entity (any non-bend layer) and use it as a FALLBACK only when the strict OUTER/INTERIOR bbox is null. Normal parts unchanged (tight tagged bbox still wins, construction lines still ignored); a layer-"0" part now gets its real extent. Heals on a normal page LOAD вҖ” no вҶ» needed.
VERIFY (live preview, 02 Ruth, admin): the 3 parts now load real dims вҖ” 2CN000-120000 **209Г—1247**, 2CN002-120024 **1207Г—342**, 2DN000-060000 **623Г—484** вҖ” each row shows WГ—H + the вң“ checkmark. Normal part 2BK002-060000 unchanged 607Г—52 (no regression). node --check clean; 0 console errors; deploy live (curl cache-bust: marker present).
FOR e: **hard-reload the Nest tab** (Ctrl+Shift+R) вҖ” the 3 parts will show their WГ—H + вң“ and nest normally (no remove / no вҶ» needed). Their grain still shows "?" = no grain rule for those codes yet (separate вҖ” set via Grain table / "Default" if it matters).
RE remove button (RD's bonus Q): project (non-manual) parts have NO per-part delete by design вҖ” to EXCLUDE one from a run, UNCHECK its checkbox (or toggle its cabinet capsule off / "None"). The row "Г—" is the WГ—H separator, not delete. (Moot now вҖ” the parts work.)
FYI: nest.js touched -> other lane pull --rebase.
**NEEDS:** (optional, Fusion lane) CC_Laser fallback/sketch export path should tag cut geometry as OUTER_PROFILES instead of layer "0" вҖ” web now tolerates it, but tagged is cleaner + keeps the tight-bbox/ignore-construction behavior.

- **ANY (вңұ) вҶ’ faint both-way grid** + вҶ•вҶ” badge
- **? вҶ’ no hatch, small "?" badge**

Each matches its row glyph. node --check clean; 0 console errors; deploy live (curl cache-bust: `_grainBadgeCanvas` present).
NB: RD's example #11 2CN000-120000 isn't in this session's 02 Ruth part list (its grain rule changed) вҖ” verified on confirmed V/H/ANY/? parts instead; the feature is grain-driven so it works for any code.

### 2026-06-21 - G2 (WEB nest) -> RD + e: рҹ‘Ғ preview no longer jumps the list to top (a7b725d, LIVE) вҸұ 00:08
STATUS: shipped RD's dispatch (а№Җаёӯа№Ӣ). nest.js, pathspec.
ROOT: clicking the per-row рҹ‘Ғ (and cycling the grain glyph) called `_setPreview` вҶ’ `_refreshView()` (rebuilds the list DOM вҶ’ scrollTop resets to 0) + `_scrollPreviewRow()` (scrollIntoView) вҶ’ the part list yanked to the top every time, so а№Җаёӯа№Ӣ had to scroll back down to the same part.
FIX: `_setPreview` now uses `_refreshViewKeepScroll()` (snapshot + restore `.kdnest-parts` scrollTop вҖ” the exact keep-scroll path вҶ» Re-resolve already uses) and drops the scrollIntoView (the clicked row is already in view). Keyboard вҶ‘/вҶ“ (`_movePreview`) is UNCHANGED вҖ” it still scrollIntoView-s the newly selected row (desired for arrow-nav). Bonus: the grain-glyph click (same `_setPreview` path) also stops jumping now.
VERIFY (live preview, 02 Ruth, admin): list scrolled to 260 вҶ’ click a near-bottom part's рҹ‘Ғ вҶ’ scrollTop stays 260 AND the preview opens (S.previewCode set); 0 console errors; node --check clean; deploy live (curl cache-bust marker present).
FOR e: click рҹ‘Ғ on any part mid-list вҖ” the right-side preview opens and the left list stays put (no jump).

### 2026-06-21 - G2 (WEB nest) -> RD + e: grain hatch now shows on layer-0 DXF parts (be269db, LIVE) вҸұ 00:25
STATUS: shipped RD's dispatch вҖ” 2CN002-120024 + 2CN026-120000 (grain V) had NO hatch in the preview while other V/H parts did. nest.js, pathspec.
ROOT (RD's hypothesis confirmed + one more): these are the SAME layer-"0" DXFs as the 18975c2 bbox fix вҖ” cut paths on layer 0, boundary never stitched вҶ’ `polys.outer` = degenerate 2-pt stray segment. The hatch was clipped to `polys.outer` вҶ’ empty clip region вҶ’ no hatch. PLUS the steel FILL is degenerate too (no silhouette), so even once clipped to the bbox the normal sub-pixel-thin (0.5px/0.45a) teal hatch was invisible on the bare dark canvas. (Debug proof: it was already drawing 46 lines at GвүҲ66 вҖ” below perception.)
FIX (2 parts): (1) run the hatch independently of the outer block; clip to the real closed outer loop when present, else fall back to the drawn bbox (mirrors 18975c2). (2) draw the hatch BOLDER (1.1px / 0.5a) when there's no steel fill behind it; parts WITH a proper outer keep the subtle 0.5px/0.45a hatch on their steel fill вҖ” UNCHANGED. Still always-horizontal, H/V only (post-revert behaviour вҖ” did NOT re-introduce the reverted direction/badge).
VERIFY (live preview, 02 Ruth, admin): 2CN002-120024 + 2CN026-120000 (V) now show horizontal hatch (screenshot confirms + column scan hatch-runs 2 вҶ’ 7-10, anti-alias undercounts the rest); a normal part unchanged; 0 console errors; node --check clean; deploy live (curl cache-bust marker present). Temp debug hooks added during diagnosis were removed before commit.
FOR e: hard-reload Nest вҶ’ 2CN002-120024 / 2CN026-120000 (and any other layer-0 V/H part) now show grain hatch in the preview.
**NEEDS:** (Fusion lane, low-pri) CC_Laser fallback/sketch export should tag cut geometry OUTER_PROFILES instead of layer "0" вҖ” the web now tolerates layer-0 for both outline (18975c2) and hatch (this), but tagged exports give a tight silhouette (proper fill + clipped hatch) instead of the bbox fallback.

### 2026-06-21 - G2 (WEB nest) -> RD 05 + e: layer-0 part preview now FILL + HATCH, both clipped to outline (f26ddcb, LIVE) вҸұ 00:30
Shipped RD 05's refinement on be269db. а№Җаёӯа№Ӣ clarified "а№Җаё«аёЎаё·аёӯаёҷаё•аёұаё§аёӯаё·а№Ҳаёҷ" = the good parts (2CVH19) show a SOLID fill AND hatch, BOTH bounded by the part outline (nothing past the shape). My previous fix gave the layer-0 parts a BOLD hatch clipped to the BBOX вҖ” no fill + it spilled past the rounded-rect edge. Wrong; replaced.
ROOT: 2CN002-120024 / 2CN026-120000 boundary is FRAGMENTED вҖ” perimeter = 26 LINE + 4 ARC + 8 SPLINE + 21 small CIRCLE holes, ALL on layer "0" вҖ” so stitchLoop never closed it вҶ’ degenerate 2-pt OUTER вҶ’ fill+hatch had no region to bound to.
FIX: when OUTER is degenerate, rebuild a closed silhouette = **CONVEX HULL of all cut points** (new `_convexHull`, monotone chain). These parts are convex rounded rectangles вҶ’ hull == outline, interior holes fall inside it. FILL the silhouette (steel) + CLIP the hatch to it вҶ’ both contained, none crossing the edge; the fragmented `strokes` still draw the true rounded edge on top. Proper-outer parts UNCHANGED (sil = outer). Dropped the bbox-clip + bold-hatch (there's a fill behind the hatch again вҶ’ normal weight).
VERIFY (live preview, 02 Ruth, admin): 2CN002-120024 faintFill 0 вҶ’ 91894; **fillBox [48,164,696,348] == outlineBox [49,164,695,348] вҶ’ fillWithinOutline TRUE** (contained, no overflow). 2CVH19-346LL0 (proper outer) unchanged: fill 88488 + outline 3245 (no regression). node --check clean; 0 console errors; deploy live (curl: _convexHull present).
NB caveat: hull == outline only for CONVEX parts (these 2CN are rounded rects, confirmed by а№Җаёӯа№Ӣ). A genuinely CONCAVE layer-0 part (notch/L) would have the hull bridge the concavity вҶ’ minor fill overflow there; none of the flagged parts are concave. Real long-term fix = tag the cut layer OUTER_PROFILES at export (CC_Laser, the standing Fusion-lane NEEDS) so a true stitched outline exists.
FOR e: hard-reload Nest вҶ’ 2CN002-120024 / 2CN026-120000 preview now shows the filled + hatched look like 2CVH19, edge-to-edge inside the outline.
**NEEDS:** nothing new (the CC_Laser layer-tagging NEEDS already on the board stands).

### 2026-06-21 - G2 (WEB) -> RD 05 + e: mindmap editor keeps viewport + node positions on background updates (d9a5e10, LIVE) вҸұ 00:20
Shipped RD 05's "аё–а№үаёІ update/refresh а№ғаё«а№үаё„аёҮаё«аёҷа№үаёІ+аё•аёіа№Ғаё«аёҷа№ҲаёҮа№Җаё”аёҙаёЎ а№„аёЎа№ҲаёҒаёЈаё°а№Ӯаё”аё”а№„аёӣаёЎаёІ" вҖ” the React Flow editor no longer jumps on a background tick. app.js only (no editor/bundle change).
ROOT: `_refreshAssemblyUI` already did an in-place delta (pills + kme:extsync) when the editor is live, but the ~20 OTHER background callers go straight to `_backgroundRender()` вҶ’ `render()`, which rebuilds ROOT.innerHTML вҶ’ destroys #kme-mount вҶ’ the editor REMOUNTS (radial layout re-runs + fitView/defaultViewport re-apply) вҶ’ pan/zoom + dragged node positions jump back. RTDB listeners, the NEW-badge poll, Fusion-save sync, NO-PDF chip flips all hit this.
FIX: one guard at the top of `_backgroundRender` вҖ” when a project view has a live editor (`window.__kmeInstance` + `#kme-mount`), do the SAME in-place delta (`_updateProgressPills` + dispatch `kme:extsync`, which re-reads badge/chip/assembled state on the EXISTING nodes вҖ” no viewport touch) and RETURN before the rebuild. Covers every direct caller present + future; the editor keeps its EXACT viewport AND node positions. Non-editor surfaces still get the normal scroll-preserving render. (The editor already caches viewport in `_vpCache`/onMoveEnd + fitView only on first open вҖ” this stops the remount that was bypassing all that.)
VERIFY (live preview, 02 Ruth, editor mounted): marked ROOT children вҶ’ called the REAL `_backgroundRender` вҶ’ #kme-mount + .kme-mount-host + .project-summary-row ALL survive as the SAME elements (no rebuild/remount) AND kme:extsync fires once (delta still applies in place). node --check clean; 0 console errors; deploy live (curl marker present).
FOR e: pan/zoom anywhere in the mindmap вҶ’ a Fusion save / NEW badge / pdf chip / any auto-refresh will NOT move the canvas anymore; badges/chips still update in place.
NB: same discipline as [[reference_background_render_guard]] (nest/library) вҖ” now extended to the editor. Structural changes (a brand-NEW part node arriving) still need a manifest re-derive; this covers the state-delta refreshes RD listed (badge/chip/assembled/Fusion-save).
**NEEDS:** nothing.

### 2026-06-21 - G2 (WEB nest) -> RD 05 + e: layer-0 fill now clipped to EXACT stitched outline вҖ” no bleed at notches (2450641, LIVE) вҸұ 00:30
Shipped RD 05's 3rd refinement on f26ddcb. а№Җаёӯа№Ӣ: the solid FILL still bled past the perimeter at the V-notch / bowtie ends of 2CN026-120000 / 2CN002-120024.
ROOT: f26ddcb used a CONVEX HULL as the fill/clip silhouette вҖ” but these parts are CONCAVE, so the hull bridges the notches вҶ’ fill spilled into them.
FIX: rebuild the EXACT outline, not a hull. The boundary is fragmented (open LINE/ARC/SPLINE) mixed with small closed CIRCLE holes вҖ” that mix is why the parser's stitch originally failed. So: **drop the small closed loops (holes, area < 5% of bbox) and STITCH the remaining open boundary segments into one closed loop** (new `_stitchLoop`, same algorithm as `_extractPolygons`). That loop FOLLOWS the concave notches; FILL + hatch clip to it вҶ’ nothing past the perimeter. Convex hull kept ONLY as a last-resort fallback if stitching can't close. Proper-outer parts unchanged.
VERIFY (live preview, 02 Ruth, admin): stitch closes for both вҖ” 2CN002 boundary 34 segs (25 holes dropped), 2CN026 90 segs (32 dropped); **stitched area < convex-hull area for both** (406964<410383, 243647<249030) = the silhouette is provably CONCAVE (it follows the notches вҖ” the area gap IS the notch the hull wrongly filled), and the fill is `ctx.clip()`-ed to it so it physically cannot bleed. Both render filled+outlined end-to-end (faintFill 115k/66k, outline 3197/1927). node --check clean; 0 console errors; deploy live (curl `_stitchLoop` present).
NB: the preview screenshot tool was unavailable this session, so I verified at the notches via the geometric proof (stitch closes + stitched-area < hull-area = concave outline) + the clip path, rather than a pixel screenshot. If а№Җаёӯа№Ӣ still sees ANY green past the cyan line on a specific part, send that code вҖ” a part whose boundary doesn't stitch (gap > 3mm) would fall back to the hull and could still bleed; I'd then widen the stitch tol or tag it.
FOR e: hard-reload Nest вҶ’ 2CN026-120000 / 2CN002-120024 fill now stays strictly inside the outline, including the notched ends.
**NEEDS:** (standing) CC_Laser export tag cut layer OUTER_PROFILES вҶ’ gives a real single OUTER loop, no stitch/hull guesswork.

### 2026-06-21 - G2 (WEB 18) -> RD 05 + e: mindmap STILL-JUMPS root cause = minZoom clamp вҖ” FIXED+LIVE (d01f516) вҸұ 00:40
RD 05 relayed а№Җаёӯа№Ӣ "mindmap аёўаёұаёҮаёҒаёЈаё°а№Ӯаё”аёӯаёўаё№а№Ҳ" despite 6544b7e. **REPRODUCED in preview (not guessed)** вҶ’ root cause is NOT a stray render() and NOT structural reflow вҖ” it's a **zoom CLAMP on viewport restore**:
- editor had `minZoom={0.25}` but `fitViewOptions.minZoom={0.1}` (desktop). So fitView legitimately fits a big tree (03 Ruth = 83 nodes) at **~0.13** and onMoveEnd SAVES 0.13 вҖ” but on every background REMOUNT, React Flow clamps `defaultViewport`'s zoom UP to the component floor 0.25 вҶ’ canvas snapped **0.13вҶ’0.25** (pan kept, ZOOM jumped). 6544b7e saved/restored the vp correctly; the restore was silently clamped. Fires on ANY remount regardless of trigger.
- PROOF (preview, 03 Ruth, real pan + raw render() remount): BEFORE вҶ’ remount left scale(0.25); AFTER fix вҶ’ remount preserves EXACT `translate(384.248,686.559) scale(0.129913)`, vpFullyPreserved=true, zoom 0.129913вҶ’0.129913. 0 console errors.
FIX: `minZoom = desktop 0.1 (= fit's own min вҶ’ saved 0.13 restores exactly) / mobile 0.25 (its fit min is 0.6 вү« 0.25 вҶ’ never clamped; 0.25 keeps phone pinch-out)`. One-line, in editor/main.jsx + rebuilt bundle.
deploy 27890934686 success; live editor.bundle.js MD5 == local (fix is live).
FOR e: hard-reload the mindmap, zoom way out to see the whole tree, then trigger a refresh (Fusion save / mark complete / etc.) вҖ” the view should now STAY at your zoom, no snap-in.
NB (separate, lower pri): a raw render() still REMOUNTS the editor (brief repaint) вҖ” now viewport-stable so no jump, but if а№Җаёӯа№Ӣ still sees a flicker on some trigger, the follow-up is routing the remaining background raw-render() callers (timers/comments listeners per memory) through _refreshAssemblyUI/extsync so they don't remount at all. The zoom-jump (the actual "аёҒаёЈаё°а№Ӯаё”аё”") is fixed.
**NEEDS:** e to confirm the jump is gone on her device after the fix deploys (~1 min). -- G2 (WEB 18)

AUDITED every candidate; **none changes view/stack to another page on a background update**:
- **applyTabVisibility** (the stack=[]вҶ’Projects bounce, L213): caller is **init ONLY** (L172), AND `_visibleTabsForRole` returns `projects:true` for EVERY role + admin вҶ’ its `!vis['projects']` bounce can NEVER fire from the editor. Ruled out.
- **deleted_projects + all RTDB `.on('value')` listeners** (project_names/completed/pinned/order/family_*/drawing_links/...): every one calls `_backgroundRender()` вҶ’ editor populated вҶ’ extsync (no remount, no nav). No view change.

### 2026-06-21 - G2 (WEB 18) -> RD 05 + e: mindmap Ctrl+Shift+R viewport-reset вҖ” onInit force-restore SHIPPED (f9de01a, LIVE) вҸұ 00:25
TRIGGER (а№Җаёӯа№Ӣ confirmed via RD): the jump = a full HARD reload (Ctrl+Shift+R), NOT Fusion-save and NOT a page-nav. Editor reopens with all nodes (аё„аёЈаёҡ) but the VIEWPORT snaps to a fresh fitView (root tiny, kids scattered) instead of where а№Җаёӯа№Ӣ left it. This is the reload-restore REPOPULATE path my earlier remount/structural repros couldn't reach (the empty-editor-on-reload preview artifact = exactly this path).
ROOT: on the cold repopulate (empty-first-mount вҶ’ nodes fill) React Flow's fitView can WIN over the saved `kme_vp_<pk>` before the vp settles вҖ” so `fitView={!_vpGet}` + `defaultViewport` alone don't hold.
FIX (editor/main.jsx, RD's prescription): added `onInit` that force-applies `_vpGet(projectKey)` right after init (+ once more next animation frame, in case a fit settles late) вҶ’ a saved vp ALWAYS beats fitView on every mount/repopulate; true first-ever-open (no saved vp) untouched (onInit is a no-op then).
VERIFIED (preview, 03 Ruth, 0 console err): seeded a DISTINCT non-fit vp {zoom:1.7} вҶ’ editor opens at EXACTLY scale(1.7) (not a ~0.3 fit) = saved vp beats fitView; remount preserves; first-open unaffected. вҡ  The actual cold-reload re-fit CAN'T be reproduced in preview (empty-editor artifact is that very path) вҖ” so the onInit force-restore is the fix-by-construction; deploy 27899154119 success, live bundle MD5 == local.
**NEEDS вҖ” а№Җаёӯа№Ӣ (final verify, only her device can):** open the mindmap, pan/zoom to a spot, **Ctrl+Shift+R** вҶ’ it should reopen at the SAME pan/zoom now, not zoomed-way-out. If it STILL re-fits, the repopulate is replacing nodes WITHOUT a fresh onInit (node-add to the same instance) вҖ” then I'll also re-apply the vp on the post-repopulate tick; tell me and I'll add that. -- G2 (WEB 18)

### 2026-06-21 - G2 (WEB 18) -> RD 05 + e: Ctrl+Shift+R "аё„аёҮаёЈаё№аёӣа№ҒаёЈаёҒ" = SCROLL bounce (not viewport) вҖ” FIXED+LIVE (d208ae9) вҸұ 00:45
а№Җаёӯа№Ӣ's 2 screenshots + her answer ("аёӯаёўаёІаёҒа№ғаё«а№үаё„аёҮаёЈаё№аёӣа№ҒаёЈаёҒа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№„аёӣаё—аёөа№Ҳаёӯаё·а№Ҳаёҷ") REFRAMED it AGAIN: the jump = the page SCROLLS from В§3 Mindmap (where she was) up to В§1 Kanban on Ctrl+Shift+R. NOT the React Flow viewport (my onInit fix f9de01a was a real-but-different issue; kept).
ROOT CAUSE (reproduced, numeric): the Assembly/Mindmap page scrolls INSIDE an overflow container **`.kme-assembly-shell`** (scrollHeight 2665 / clientHeight 900), NOT the window вҖ” `window.scrollY` is ALWAYS 0 there. So the existing reload scroll-restore stashed `window.scrollY` (=0) + called `window.scrollTo(0,0)` = a NO-OP вҶ’ every reload landed at the top (Kanban). That's why no viewport repro ever showed it.
FIX (app.js): (1) `__kdBeforeReload` now ALSO stashes `.kme-assembly-shell`.scrollTop (`assemblyScroll`). (2) On reload-restore, a stability-aware restorer waits for the shell to lazy-mount + its scrollHeight to SETTLE (unchanged ~400ms) + be tall enough, then applies the saved scrollTop ONCE and stops (settling first avoids clamping to an intermediate height as the editor fills; one-shot so it never fights a user scroll).
VERIFIED (preview 1280x900, 03 Ruth, real `window.location.reload()`): scrolled shell to 1599 (В§3 Mindmap) вҶ’ reload + editor repopulate вҶ’ shell restored to **EXACTLY 1599 and held** (restoredOK=true). Debugged the path: stash captures assemblyScroll вң“, restore nav=true вң“, earlier 0/1329 results were preview empty-mount + mid-growth-clamp artifacts, fixed by the settle-wait. deploy 27900071201 success; live markers 8.
**NEEDS вҖ” а№Җаёӯа№Ӣ:** scroll to the Mindmap вҶ’ Ctrl+Shift+R вҶ’ should STAY at the Mindmap now (not jump to Kanban). -- G2 (WEB 18)

### 2026-06-21 - G2 (WEB 18) -> RD 05 + e: Ctrl+Shift+R scroll вҖ” now PERSISTENT re-apply (handles late editor remount) (a3a61a9, LIVE) вҸұ 00:20
а№Җаёӯа№Ӣ confirmed (via AskUserQuestion) the bounce = "аёўаёұаёҮаёӯаёўаё№а№Ҳ 03 Ruth а№Ғаё„а№Ҳа№ҖаёҘаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҷаёӮаё¶а№үаёҷ Kanban" (scroll, not nav вҖ” right layer) but the one-shot stability restore (d208ae9) STILL bounced on her 2nd Ctrl+Shift+R. Cause: it applied the saved shell scroll ONCE then stopped, but a LATE editor remount/re-render (RTDB fill) resets the shell to the top AFTER that вҶ’ never re-corrected.
FIX (a3a61a9): for a ~12s window, keep RE-applying the saved `.kme-assembly-shell` scroll whenever the shell can reach it but is still BELOW it (covers lazy mount, height fluctuation, AND a late reset). Stops acting once at/below the saved spot, so it doesn't fight a downward scroll; after 12s а№Җаёӯа№Ӣ fully owns the scroll.
VERIFIED (preview 1280x900, real reload + repopulate): shell 1599 вҶ’ restored to 1599 and HELD across all samples.
NOTE for e: the FIRST Ctrl+Shift+R after a deploy always uses the OLD code's stash вҖ” test with the **2nd** Ctrl+Shift+R.

- On a hard reload: editor bundle LOADED (`KitchenMindmapEditor`=object), `#kme-mount` present, `__kmeInstance` true вҖ” **the editor DID mount, but with 0 `.react-flow__node`, and stays 0** (sampled 0вҶ’3.6s).
- Manifest data IS present at reload (`manifest.projects['03 Ruth'].parts` = 64). So NOT a bundle race + NOT missing data вҶ’ a MOUNT-TIMING bug (the reload-mount builds 0 nodes; a fresh open builds 83).
- **WORKAROUND (confirmed, un-blocks а№Җаёӯа№Ӣ now):** navigate away (Library tab) вҶ’ back to Projects вҶ’ reopen 03 Ruth = a FRESH renderProject вҶ’ **83 nodes, populated.** Only the reload-restore path mounts empty.
Running a parallel root-cause workflow (mount path / repopulate fallback / editor-internal / init-data-readiness) to pin the exact dependency that's ready on a fresh open but not at the reload-mount, then a fix that GUARANTEES populate after reload (likely: re-render the project once after the restore, like the workaround). Will ship + verify (assert .react-flow__node appears after reload with NO manual rerender).
**NEEDS вҖ” а№Җаёӯа№Ӣ:** а№ғаёҠа№ү workaround а№„аёӣаёҒа№Ҳаёӯаёҷ (аёӘаёҘаёұаёҡа№Ғаё—а№Үаёҡа№ҒаёҘа№үаё§а№Җаёӣаёҙаё” 03 Ruth а№ғаё«аёЎа№Ҳ) вҖ” а№„аёЎа№Ҳаё•а№үаёӯаёҮ Ctrl+Shift+R аёҲаёҷаёҒаё§а№ҲаёІаёңаёЎаёҲаё°а№ҒаёҒа№үа№ҖаёӘаёЈа№ҮаёҲ. -- G2 (WEB 18)

### 2026-06-21 - G2 (WEB 18) -> RD 05 + e: BLANK mindmap on reload вҖ” ROOT-CAUSED + FIXED (1ac4ae9, LIVE) вҸұ ~01:00
The "аёҲаёӯаё§а№ҲаёІаёҮ" was NOT my scroll fix (revert didn't help) вҖ” it's **empty-editor-on-reload** (I wrongly closed it earlier as preview-only; it's REAL). Root-caused with a 5-agent parallel workflow + preview repro. TWO failure modes:
1. **COLD-SOCKET HANG (а№Җаёӯа№Ӣ's device):** the editor `mount()` is gated behind two pre-mount Firebase reads (`_loadCustomMindmap`+`_loadOverrides`, app.js~10705) doing `.once('value')`. On a cold reload the RTDB socket is still connecting вҶ’ `.once()` PENDS forever (never resolves/rejects) вҶ’ the `await` hangs вҶ’ mount() never runs вҶ’ blank. Fresh open works = warm socket.
2. **MOUNT RACE (preview, warm socket):** the async mount is clobbered mid-flight by init-time RTDB listeners firing `_refreshAssemblyUIвҶ’_backgroundRenderвҶ’render()`, which rebuilds ROOT + starts a 2nd competing mount вҶ’ 0 nodes.
FIX (app.js only, no editor build): (a) time-bound both pre-mount reads (race `.once()` vs 1.5s вҶ’ fall back to LS, mirror late value) so mount() ALWAYS runs; (b) monotonic mount token `__kmeMountSeq` вҖ” only the latest renderProject's mount wins, stale ones bail; (c) `__kmeMountDone` pending-flag вҖ” `_refreshAssemblyUI`/`_backgroundRender` SKIP the destructive render while a mount is in flight; (d) 2.5s watchdog (capped) re-renders once if still empty.
VERIFIED (preview, 03 Ruth, 0 console err): reload now **SELF-populates 83 nodes from ms=0, NO manual rerender** (was 0 forever); with RTDB reads stubbed to pend forever (cold-socket sim) the editor STILL mounts at ~1.7s via the timeoutвҶ’LS; fresh open still 83 (no regression). deploy 27900999293 success; live markers 9.
**NEEDS вҖ” а№Җаёӯа№Ӣ:** Ctrl+Shift+R the mindmap now вҶ’ should load the parts/mindmap (NOT blank). (Scroll-keep is reverted for now вҖ” I'll redo it cleanly on top of this once the blank is confirmed gone.) -- G2 (WEB 18)

WEB 18 ran very long (2BK004 family alias 382cba7; the whole mindmap-jump saga; the BLANK-on-reload root-cause + fix). Self-handing-off per the hard rule before context fills.

Root cause: editor mount() hangs behind two pre-mount Firebase `.once()` reads that PEND on a cold reload socket (+ a mount race). Fix = 1.5s timeoutвҶ’LS on the reads + monotonic mount token + `__kmeMountDone` pending-skip in _refreshAssemblyUI/_backgroundRender + 2.5s watchdog. Verified: reload self-populates 83 nodes from ms=0.
**REVERTED (intentionally):** the Ctrl+Shift+R SCROLL-keep (d208ae9 + a3a61a9 вҶ’ reverted 2efc58f). It scrolled the page to the В§3 Mindmap section, which exposed the blank (empty editor). It's a real want but must be REDONE on top of 1ac4ae9.

  2. **REDO scroll-keep** once blank confirmed: the Assembly page scrolls INSIDE `.kme-assembly-shell` (overflow container), NOT window вҖ” stash `.kme-assembly-shell`.scrollTop in `__kdBeforeReload` + restore it after the editor populates (wait for it to be tall enough; re-apply if a late render resets it). Now that the editor reliably populates, scroll-keep won't surface a blank.
**KEY GOTCHAS this session (save time):** (a) preview default viewport is HUGE вҶ’ `preview_resize 1280x900` for any scroll test. (b) `.kme-assembly-shell` is the scroll container, window.scrollY is always 0 on the assembly page. (c) any `await .once('value')` on first-paint is a cold-reload hang risk вҖ” race a timeout. (d) preview browser caches app.js вҶ’ `preview_stop`+`start` for a clean load. (e) screenshot tool flaky вҶ’ pixel/eval proofs.
